name: Encoder-decoder (bottleneck)
author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: Block-level encoder-decoder 'hourglass': input block, encoder trapezoid narrowing to a latent bottleneck z, decoder trapezoid widening to an output block. Parametric sizes and labels.

parameters.name: \blockH
parameters.meaning: height of the input/output blocks; also each trapezoid's wide (outer) edge

parameters.name: \latentH
parameters.meaning: height of the bottleneck z; also each trapezoid's narrow (inner) edge, so shapes meet z flush

parameters.name: \gap
parameters.meaning: horizontal gap between parts (also the arrow length)

parameters.name: \latentlabel
parameters.meaning: bottleneck label drawn inside z (keep short, e.g. $z$)

edit_contract.node_naming: fixed semantic node names: (input) (enc) (z) (dec) (output); (enc)/(dec) are the centre text nodes, not the trapezoid paths

operations.summary: edit the matching label macro; no layout change needed
change the palette name in the encoder/decoder \filldraw or the latentblk style; never a hex/stock color
\blockH/\blockW for I/O blocks, \trapW for trapezoid length, \latentH/\latentW for the bottleneck, \gap for spacing
place a palette-colored block with positioning above (z) that (enc) feeds and that feeds (dec); connect with flow arrows; keep (enc)/(z)/(dec) intact
insert another running edge pair after the relevant part, push later edges along by its width + \gap, draw the node and an extra flow arrow; keep every part centred on y=0

invariants: keep each trapezoid's inner-edge height equal to \latentH so encoder/decoder stay flush with z
colors only via the five palette names (tints/shades allowed); never inline hex or stock colors
